name: Gemini 3.6 Flash
summary: Code and run agents on a cheaper workhorse model — $1.50 per million input tokens, output priced well under the old Flash tier, and 17% fewer output tokens per job. Live in the API and GitHub Copilot now.

name: Buzz
summary: Run a free, open-source team chat that treats AI agents as real members with their own identity and permissions. Includes code hosting and automations, and plugs into Claude Code, Codex, or goose. Mac, Windows, Linux.

name: Project Indigo AI playground
summary: Get an LLM critique of your photos and pull clutter out of shots — background people, wires, poles, trash cans, or anything you describe. It lives in a free iOS camera app, still test-phase for select users.

name: Kimi K3
summary: Use a 2.8T multimodal coding model today through Kimi.com, Kimi Work, Kimi Code, or the API. It has a 1M-token context window, with downloadable weights promised by July 27.

name: Codex Micro
summary: Run a fleet of coding agents from a $230 limited-run desk keyboard. Light-up keys show agent status, a joystick launches workflows, and a dial sets how hard an agent thinks. It pairs with the ChatGPT desktop app.

name: Inkling
summary: Fine-tune or download a 975B open-weight multimodal model with a lighter Inkling-Small preview for cheaper experiments. Tinker handles customization when local hardware is unrealistic.

name: GPT-5.6
summary: Build with Sol, Terra, or Luna in ChatGPT, Codex, and the API. Lower-cost tiers make agentic app builds and long Codex runs more affordable, while ChatGPT Work carries projects across files and apps.
